speaker: HELEN
text: But this exceeding posting day and night

speaker: HELEN
text: Must wear your spirits low. We cannot help it.

speaker: HELEN
text: But since you have made the days and nights as one

speaker: HELEN
text: To wear your gentle limbs in my affairs,

speaker: HELEN
text: Be bold you do so grow in my requital

speaker: HELEN
text: As nothing can unroot you.

speaker: HELEN
text: In happy time!

speaker: HELEN
text: This man may help me to his Majesty’s ear,

speaker: HELEN
text: If he would spend his power.—God save you, sir.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: And you.

speaker: HELEN
text: Sir, I have seen you in the court of France.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: I have been sometimes there.

speaker: HELEN
text: I do presume, sir, that you are not fall’n

speaker: HELEN
text: From the report that goes upon your goodness,

speaker: HELEN
text: And therefore, goaded with most sharp occasions

speaker: HELEN
text: Which lay nice manners by, I put you to

speaker: HELEN
text: The use of your own virtues, for the which

speaker: HELEN
text: I shall continue thankful.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: What’s your will?

speaker: HELEN
text: That it will please you

speaker: HELEN
text: To give this poor petition to the King

speaker: HELEN
text: And aid me with that store of power you have

speaker: HELEN
text: To come into his presence.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: The King’s not here.

speaker: HELEN
text: Not here, sir?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Not indeed.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: He hence removed last night, and with more haste

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Than is his use.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Lord, how we lose our pains!

speaker: HELEN
text: All’s well that ends well yet,

speaker: HELEN
text: Though time seem so adverse and means unfit.—

speaker: HELEN
text: I do beseech you, whither is he gone?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Marry, as I take it, to Rossillion,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Whither I am going.

speaker: HELEN
text: I do beseech you, sir,

speaker: HELEN
text: Since you are like to see the King before me,

speaker: HELEN
text: Commend the paper to his gracious hand,

speaker: HELEN
text: Which I presume shall render you no blame

speaker: HELEN
text: But rather make you thank your pains for it.

speaker: HELEN
text: I will come after you with what good speed

speaker: HELEN
text: Our means will make us means.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: This I’ll do for you.

speaker: HELEN
text: And you shall find yourself to be well thanked

speaker: HELEN
text: Whate’er falls more. We must to horse again.—

speaker: HELEN
text: Go, go, provide.
